title: common: no slirp for podman6
body:
<!--- Please read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/containers/container-libs/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) before proceeding --->

See individual commits.

Vendored into https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/28451 and https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/6443

comments.body: (I didn’t _read_ the changes yet) it looks like this would benefit from rebasing now that #417 is merged.
Rebased. Good for another look. Thanks.
Best to proceed with this only after initial obvious removals from Podman repo.
Opening for review. @containers/container-libs-maintainers PTAL . See description for links to podman and buildah vendor dance PRs. Keeping those in draft until we merge this.

If we remove code from c/common while it is still used in Buildah/Podman, that might make it impossible to vendor container-libs by _others_ working on unrelated features in the meantime, like the config file rework.

Is there any easy way to split/sequence the work to never break vendoring in the meantime, perhaps removing tests+consumers immediately, _then_ removing the called code? (I didn’t check _at all_ whether this is possible/easy.) Or perhaps that already happened?
I agree I think the buildah and podman sides looks safe to merge before the PR here as they only remove the API calls. This would definitely help others doing breaking changes at the same time.
I can redo the buildah and podman PRs without the vendoring changes and defer this one until we're safe to proceed.
